_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Assess efficiency of existing information exchange and management systems.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in conducting operational process improvement for data workflows, identifying inefficiencies, designing automation opportunities, and measuring improvement outcomes.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI OP domain. OP-S-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: OP-A-002 (OP, cosine: 0.421), DG-T-005 (DG, cosine: 0.402).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 5.6/10 (raw 0.556). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [assess, efficiency, exchange, existing, information, management] vs WIDAI-only terms [automation, conducting, data, designing, identifying, improvement] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.880 (P=0.887, R=0.873). Balanced P/R — the 9-word DCWF element and 18-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.5%). "Assess efficiency of existing information exchange and management systems." and "Skill in conducting operational process improvement for data workflows, identify" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.424 < STS 0.556 (gap -0.132). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [assess, efficiency, exchange] and [automation, conducting, data] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [assess, efficiency, exchange, existing, information, management]. WIDAI terms: [automation, conducting, data, designing, identifying, improvement].
